title: Preparing for the worst: RAF Lossiemouth tests response to airfield emergency

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: RAF personnel trained closely with members of the emergency services RAF Lossiemouth has been testing its capability to deal with an emergency incident while continuing to protect the UK northern airspace. Personnel have been training alongside civilian emergency services on the base as part of Exercise Agile Warrior. They have practised responding to a serious incident on the base involving multiple casualties and a fuel spillage from a tanker involved in the collision.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: RAF trials back-up base as Typhoons and Poseidons operate out of Kinloss Knocked out, on fire or crashed: RAF Lossiemouth trains for worst-case scenarios Quick Reaction Alert: How the RAF protects our skies 24 hours a day, 365 days a year It is part of wider training for RAF Lossiemouth, making sure the personnel can carry out their jobs under pressure.
